Operation 'getApiV1Ping' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, not_actionable. Original summary and description are repetitive and lack clarity about the operation's purpose as a health check. The description doesn't explain what 'pong' response means or how to interpret success. Improved version clarifies this is a health/connectivity check, explains the authentication validation, and provides actionable guidance for AI agents.

data.current_summary: Validates the request is authenticated and server is up and running.
data.current_description:
This request simply validates the authentication and server is up and running by going through
the authentication process and returning the pong if successful.
data.summary_suggestion: Check API authentication and server health
data.description_suggestion: Verifies that the API credentials (x-api-key and x-api-secret headers) are valid and the Easybizy server is operational. Returns a successful response if authentication passes and the server is running. Use this operation to validate API connectivity and credential validity before executing other operations.
data.reasoning: Original summary and description are repetitive and lack clarity about the operation's purpose as a health check. The description doesn't explain what 'pong' response means or how to interpret success. Improved version clarifies this is a health/connectivity check, explains the authentication validation, and provides actionable guidance for AI agents.

message: Operation 'getApiV1Publicping' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, unclear_purpose, missing_context. The 'public-ping' endpoint name suggests it differs from the authenticated ping, but the current description is identical to the authenticated version, creating confusion. The improved version clarifies that this is a public/unauthenticated health check, distinguishes it from the authenticated ping endpoint, and explains when to use each operation.

data.current_summary: Validates the request is authenticated and server is up and running.
data.current_description:
This request simply validates the authentication and server is up and running by going through
the authentication process and returning the pong if successful.
data.description_suggestion: Performs a public health check on the Easybizy server without requiring API authentication. Returns a successful response if the server is operational. Unlike the /api/v1/ping endpoint, this operation does not validate credentials. Use this to verify server availability or for monitoring purposes when authentication is not available.
data.reasoning: The 'public-ping' endpoint name suggests it differs from the authenticated ping, but the current description is identical to the authenticated version, creating confusion. The improved version clarifies that this is a public/unauthenticated health check, distinguishes it from the authenticated ping endpoint, and explains when to use each operation.

message: Operation 'postApiV1Calllogs' has semantic quality issues: missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. The current description provides good input examples but lacks clarity on what the operation returns and the side effects of the popup notification. The improved version consolidates the examples into a clearer format, specifies required vs optional fields, clarifies the popup behavior as a side effect, and indicates what is returned.

data.current_summary: Log an incoming/answered call to the system.
data.current_description:
Creates a new incoming call log on Easybizy.
If current logged-in user on Easybizy is not blocked to receive incoming calls, a popup screen will be displayed on easybizy.

A simple body might be: ``{ "phoneNumber": "0504445554"}``

A body with extension: ``{ "phoneNumber": "0504445554", "extension": "255"}``

A body with uid, that is the external unique id: ``{ "phoneNumber": "0504445554", "extension": "255", "uid": "33fdbee4-6512-4a48-8eb2-68000e8a962a"}``

A body with extension and status: ``{ "phoneNumber": "0504445554", "extension": "255", "status": "ANSWER"}`` - Default status ``NEWCALL``

For a more descripitive model, have a look at the Model section on the description - following example.
data.description_suggestion: Creates a new incoming call log in Easybizy. Required field: phoneNumber. Optional fields: extension (call routing), uid (external unique identifier), and status (defaults to NEWCALL; can be set to ANSWER). If the logged-in user is not blocked from receiving calls, a popup notification will display in Easybizy. Returns the created call log record. Refer to the Call log details model for complete field specifications.
data.reasoning: The current description provides good input examples but lacks clarity on what the operation returns and the side effects of the popup notification. The improved version consolidates the examples into a clearer format, specifies required vs optional fields, clarifies the popup behavior as a side effect, and indicates what is returned.

message: Operation 'postApiV1Giftcards' has semantic quality issues: missing_output_details, poor_ai_interpretability. The current description is comprehensive but difficult for AI agents to parse due to length and complexity. The summary doesn't capture the dual-path nature of the operation. The improved summary clarifies the two execution modes, and the description is reorganized for clarity: execution paths first, then required/optional fields, then return values, with the critical warning preserved. This helps AI agents understand when and how to use this operation.

data.current_summary: Creates a gift card payable link.
data.current_description:
Returns a payment link for a gift card or directly creates and sends a gift card based on the `priceAfterDiscount` value.
If `priceAfterDiscount` is `null` or `0`, the gift card will be immediately created and sent (if sending has been requested on the `recipient` field), and the response will include the generated gift card details. In this case, the `ignorePayment` field must be set to `true`.
If `priceAfterDiscount` is greater than `0`, a payment link will be generated, and upon successful payment, the gift card will be created.
This method is highly flexible yet can be simplified. The minimum required fields for an immediate gift card issuance can be as simple as:
``{"giftCardCreditAmount": 500, "buyer": {"name": "John Doe", "phoneNumber": "0508116811"}, "ignorePayment": true}``

If a successful payment webhook is required, the `paymentSuccessWebhook` (see the model for a description) field should be set. The webhook supports both `get` and `post` methods, if a method is not provided, it will default to `post`. When `get` is used, the payload will be appended to the URL as query parameters. Supported fields are: `paymentLinkId`, `giftCardUsageAmount`, `amountCharged`, and `cardLastFourDigits`, and any additional `constants` that are required (that will be simply attached to the payload/url). If a different field mapping is required, it can be provided in the `fieldsMap` field. As and example `{"fieldsMap": {"paymentLinkId": "paymentId"}}` will map the easybizy's field `paymentLinkId` to your `paymentId` field in the payload/url. If unsupported fields are provided, they will be ignored.
For a more detailed model, refer to the Model section in the description, following the example below.

⚠️ **Important Notice:** If `priceAfterDiscount` is `null` or `0`, the system will **immediately create and send** a gift card. This effectively means issuing real money, so extra caution must be taken to ensure correctness and prevent unintended issuance. Using this direct method, requires CREATE permission for the gift-card entity.
data.summary_suggestion: Create gift card with payment link or immediate issuance
data.description_suggestion: Creates a gift card in Easybizy with two execution paths based on priceAfterDiscount: (1) If priceAfterDiscount is null/0 with ignorePayment=true, immediately creates and sends the gift card (requires CREATE permission); (2) If priceAfterDiscount > 0, generates a payment link that triggers gift card creation upon successful payment. Required fields: giftCardCreditAmount, buyer (name and phoneNumber). Optional: extension, uid, status, recipient, paymentSuccessWebhook. Webhook supports GET/POST methods with customizable field mapping. Returns either gift card details (immediate issuance) or payment link (payment required). ⚠️ Immediate issuance issues real value—verify all details before execution.
data.reasoning: The current description is comprehensive but difficult for AI agents to parse due to length and complexity. The summary doesn't capture the dual-path nature of the operation. The improved summary clarifies the two execution modes, and the description is reorganized for clarity: execution paths first, then required/optional fields, then return values, with the critical warning preserved. This helps AI agents understand when and how to use this operation.

message: Operation 'postApiV1Leads' has semantic quality issues: missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. The current description mentions the deduplication behavior (similar mobile phone) but doesn't clearly explain it as a side effect or merge operation. It also lacks information about what the operation returns. The improved version clarifies the deduplication/merge behavior as a side effect, specifies required vs recommended fields, explains the validation rule more clearly, and indicates what is returned.

data.current_description:
Creates a new lead on Easybizy.
In case of similar mobile phone, details will be added to the existing lead.
A lead request must have either or mobile | email (if none the request will not pass through).

A simple example might be: ``{ "Name": "Haim Cohen", "Mobile":"0506655441" ,"ArrivalSource": "Facebook", "TagsStr":"Facial-Campign,Ad 1332344"}``

For a more descripitive model, have a look at the Model section on the description - following example.
data.description_suggestion: Creates a new lead in Easybizy or updates an existing lead if a matching mobile phone number is found. Required: at least one of mobile or email must be provided. Recommended fields: Name, ArrivalSource (e.g., Facebook), TagsStr (comma-separated tags for categorization). If a lead with the same mobile number exists, the provided details will be added/merged to that lead instead of creating a duplicate. Returns the created or updated lead record. Refer to the Lead details model for complete field specifications.
data.reasoning: The current description mentions the deduplication behavior (similar mobile phone) but doesn't clearly explain it as a side effect or merge operation. It also lacks information about what the operation returns. The improved version clarifies the deduplication/merge behavior as a side effect, specifies required vs recommended fields, explains the validation rule more clearly, and indicates what is returned.
